content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 16, 2002

David Whitsell, Chief
Ethics and Operational Compliance Office
Department of Insurance
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 1300
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-248

Dear Mr. Whitsell:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the California Department of Insurance ("department") regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

Your request pertains to a department employee's private employment as an independent contractor providing translation services. Specifically, you are concerned that the translation services may be an activity incompatible with the employee's duties as a research program specialist within the department's fraud division. As such, your request does not present a question under the provisions of the Act. Pursuant to your telephone conversation with our office on October 15, 2002, this letter confirms that you are withdrawing your request for advice.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Kenneth L. Glick
Counsel, Legal Division

KLG:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\02-248

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
